title: practical english usage - Part 194
content:
I always take an umbrella in case it rains. (= ... because it might rain.) which  we  do in order to be ready for possible future situations.

In British English, in case is mostly used to talk about precautions - things 1  precautions 248 in   case     and   if                        _ not I’ ll be in all day unless the office phones, (     .. .unless the office will phone. ) (►   231).

In clauses with unless, we  usually use present tenses to refer to the future 3 tenses grammar •  Section 23   Other Adverbial Clauses 253  no matter who, etc 252  whoever, whatever, etc                               subjects, etc simultaneous events                       256  than- and ɑs-elauses: leaving out 251  as, when and while:           word order 250 before: conjunction                     255  as and though: special 249  after, conjunction                       254  whether... or...

Less problematic conjunctions This Section deals with some conjunctions and clause structures which  may INTRODUCTION Section 23   Other Adverbial Clauses grammar •  249  after: conjunction I go for a walk before I have breakfast.

Before I have breakfast, I go for a walk.

information structure (►  267).

comma) or after. Putting it at the end can give it more importance in Before and its clause can come  either before the main clause (often with a 1  use   and   position clause + before + clause before + clause, + clause 250 before:      conjunction For after as an adverb, ►    360.

He wrote his first book after returning / having returned from Mongolia.

After having + past participle is also possible when talking about the past.

After completing this form, give it to the secretary.

In a formal style, we  often use the structure after + -ing.

I'll call you after I've seen Jake. (     .. . after I'll have seen Jake.) We use a present perfect, not a future perfect, to talk about the future with after.

After Lara had finished school, she went to America.

I usually go straight home after I've finished work.

one thing is completed before another starts (►   54.1).

In clauses with after, we  often use present and past perfect tenses to show that 3  perfect      tenses not I'll telephone you after I arrive. (     ... after I will arrive.) We   use after with a present tense to talk about the future (►  231).

2  present      with    future     meaning I moved to Scotland after I changed jobs. (The speaker changed jobs first.) then changed jobs. Compare: Note the time relations: in both cases the speaker moved to Scotland first and I changed jobs after I moved to Scotland.

After I moved to Scotland, I changed jobs.
